skill.name: commit-drafter
skill.description: Drafts commit message context from staged Git changes, including status, statistics, and full diff content.
skill.summary: Generate reviewable commit message context from staged Git changes.
skill.author: abereyes

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are confirmed as intentional Git usage with fixed argument lists, not shell injection. The weak-cryptography findings are false positives on prose and AI instruction text. A medium risk remains because full staged diff content is placed into the model context, where malicious diff text could influence the drafted message.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Diff Content Enters AI Prompt
medium_findings.description: The script retrieves the full staged diff and places it directly before instructions for the AI. A staged file can contain adversarial text that influences the generated commit message, although the script does not execute that text.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly inserts full_diff into the output consumed by the AI. The risk depends on repository content, so this is a prompt-context concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Fixed Git Subprocess Usage
low_findings.description: The subprocess.run finding is real command execution, but it invokes git with list arguments and no shell. The observed calls use fixed Git subcommands needed for the skill purpose, so command injection evidence was not found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call uses argv form rather than shell parsing, and the command lists are constructed by local functions. The SKILL.md hit is prose that mentions git diff, not an executable instruction by itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations contain skill description text, Claude references, and commit-message instructions. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or encryption code is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows ordinary prose and generated-message instructions at every reported location. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related weak cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The script runs Git commands through subprocess.run to inspect repository state. This is necessary for the feature, but it means the skill depends on local command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: External command execution is directly present, but arguments are fixed and shell execution is not used. This lowers injection risk while preserving an operational risk factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Full Diff Included In Model Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The script appends full staged diff text to the output that guides the AI response. Repository changes may include secrets or prompt-like text that should be reviewed before relying on the output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from git diff to AI-facing output is explicit in the code. The impact is contextual because the content depends on staged changes.

content.value_statement: Writing useful commit messages from staged changes takes time and context switching. This skill collects Git status, statistics, and diff context so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can draft a reviewable message.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the current directory is a Git repository.
Detects staged added, modified, deleted, and renamed files.
Reads staged diff statistics for changed files.
Includes the full staged diff for AI analysis.
Outputs structured instructions for drafting a commit message.
limitations: Requires the Git command line tool and a local Git repository.
Does not stage files, create commits, or push changes.
Diff content may include sensitive data or prompt-like text.

use_cases.title: Summarize staged work before commit
use_cases.description: Generate a clear first draft from the exact files and diff content already staged.

use_cases.title: Standardize commit message structure
use_cases.description: Ask for consistent summaries with What, Why, and How sections for team review.

use_cases.title: Review change intent before handoff
use_cases.description: Use the generated context to explain staged changes before passing work to another contributor.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a basic commit message
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple reviewable message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a commit message for my staged changes. Keep the summary concise and include the main reason for the change.

prompt_templates.title: Use a structured format
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants consistent team commit messages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a commit message from my staged changes with a summary line and What, Why, and How sections.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on user impact
prompt_templates.scenario: Product or feature work needs a clearer change explanation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a commit message that explains the user-visible impact of my staged changes and separates implementation details from behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a polished maintainer message
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for final review before committing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my staged diff and draft a polished commit message. Mention risky areas, avoid overstating intent, and flag anything I should review before committing.

output_examples.input: Staged changes add validation to a form and update related tests.
output_examples.output:
Add form validation checks

What: Adds validation rules and updates test coverage.
Why: Prevents invalid input from reaching the application flow.
How: Applies validation before submission and verifies expected error states.

output_examples.input: Staged changes modify documentation and rename a helper file.
output_examples.output:
Update documentation and helper naming

What: Refreshes usage notes and renames the helper file.
Why: Makes the workflow easier to follow.
How: Aligns file naming with the documented command flow.

best_practices: Review the staged diff before asking for a draft.
Check the generated message for accuracy before committing.
Remove secrets or unrelated changes from the staging area first.
anti_patterns: Do not use generated commit text without reviewing it.
Do not stage unrelated changes and expect a focused message.
Do not rely on this skill to detect security issues in the diff.

faq.question: Does this skill create the commit?
faq.answer: No. It drafts commit message context and prints it for review. You still create the commit yourself.

faq.question: Does it analyze unstaged changes?
faq.answer: No. It only reads staged changes from the Git index.

faq.question: Can it work outside a Git repository?
faq.answer: No. It returns an error when the current directory is not a Git repository.

faq.question: Does it send data over the network?
faq.answer: No network call is present in the reviewed files. The skill prints local Git context for the AI tool to use.

faq.question: What should I check before using it?
faq.answer: Check that staged changes do not include secrets, unrelated files, or text intended to manipulate AI instructions.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
